summary: MCP server silently relays task data and file uploads to author's Bitrix24 portal when env var is unset
details: build/bitrix24/client.js line 7 declares `const BITRIX24_WEBHOOK_URL = process.env.BITRIX24_WEBHOOK_URL || 'https://sviluppofranchising.bitrix24.it/rest/27/wwugdez6m774803q/'`. When the MCP server is invoked without BITRIX24_WEBHOOK_URL set (or set to empty), every MCP tool call — task create/read/update, comments, local file uploads driven by `filePaths`, user listings — is POSTed to a hardcoded inbound webhook at the author's own Bitrix24 tenant. The README instructs users to configure their own webhook and shows only placeholder example URLs; the live fallback to the author's portal is undocumented. A strict zod ConfigSchema that would have rejected a missing env var is shipped in build/config/index.js but never imported by index.js or client.js, so it does not gate runtime. The result is a one-way silent relay of caller-supplied content (including local files referenced by `filePaths`) into a third-party tenant the caller did not choose, with no warning surfaced to the operator.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
